title_en: Zakah and Siyam — Alms and Fasting
content_en:
This chapter addresses two of the five pillars of Islam: zakah (obligatory alms) and siyam (fasting in Ramadan). Sheikh al-Fawzan treats them together as foundational acts of worship, one financial and one physical, both requiring careful understanding of their conditions and rulings.

On zakah, the author begins with its textual foundations and the scholarly consensus on its obligation. He explains the nisab — the minimum threshold of wealth that triggers the obligation — for each category of zakatable property: gold and silver (and by extension cash and trade goods), livestock (camels, cattle, and sheep), and agricultural produce. For each category, he specifies the nisab, the haul (the lunar year that must elapse before zakah is due), and the percentage or quantity to be paid. The chapter clarifies that the zakah on trade goods is calculated on their market value at the time of payment, a ruling of great practical importance for contemporary Muslims engaged in commerce.

The recipients of zakah are enumerated according to the Quranic verse (9:60): the fuqara (poor), the masakin (destitute), those employed to collect and distribute zakah, those whose hearts are to be reconciled, those in bondage, those burdened with debt, those striving in the path of Allah, and the wayfarer. Sheikh al-Fawzan discusses eligibility conditions for each category and the prohibition against giving zakah to one's immediate dependents or to non-Muslims in most cases.

On siyam, the author covers the conditions for the obligation to fast: being Muslim, having reached puberty, being sane, and being resident and healthy. He explains the acts that invalidate the fast (eating, drinking, sexual intercourse, and others), distinguishing between those that require only a qada (make-up day) and those that require both qada and kaffarah (expiation). The chapter covers the rulings on the person who is ill, the traveler, the pregnant and nursing woman, and the elderly, explaining when each may break the fast and how they compensate. Voluntary fasting and the most meritorious days for it are mentioned in conclusion.
title_id: Zakat dan Puasa — Kewajiban Harta dan Ibadah Shaum
content_id:
Bab ini membahas dua dari lima rukun Islam: zakat (sedekah wajib) dan puasa Ramadan. Syekh al-Fawzan memperlakukannya bersama sebagai tindakan ibadah yang mendasar — satu bersifat finansial, satu bersifat jasmani — yang keduanya diwajibkan atas setiap Muslim yang memenuhi syarat.

Pembahasan zakat mencakup: nisab dan haul berbagai jenis harta (emas, perak, uang, perniagaan, pertanian, ternak, rikaz), delapan golongan yang berhak menerima zakat, zakat fitrah, dan berbagai masalah kontemporer. Pembahasan puasa mencakup: wajibnya puasa Ramadan, penetapan awal Ramadan, niat, hal-hal yang membatalkan puasa, kafarat, orang-orang yang mendapat keringanan (musafir, sakit, hamil, menyusui, lansia), qadha, puasa sunnah (Syawal, Arafah, Asyura, Senin-Kamis, dll.), hari-hari yang terlarang berpuasa, dan itikaf.
